rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees referring to ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Preserve AADL component structure and scope rules when inserting AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Use component type annexes for high-level assumptions and guarantees.
Use implementation annexes for low-level bindings and assignments.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE contracts are declared or in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be declared in appropriate AADL scopes.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without proper scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
